title: arceuthobium globosum subsp. grandicaule
intro: Arceuthobium globosum subsp. grandicaule, also known as the grand mistletoe or American dwarf mistletoe, is a parasitic plant that grows on the branches of various tree species, particularly spruce and fir. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, attributed to the presence of alkaloids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Arceuthobium globosum subsp. grandicaule are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
